commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=9984a0d2521cdcfe5d58edc93790a7da933e430a)
commands.review_summary:
The PR expands the ACP manager stale persistent-session recovery matcher to include `Internal error` and adds a mock regression test for a Kiro-shaped resume failure.

PR surface: Source 0, Tests +105. Total +105 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: the linked issue provides exact Kiro `SessionResumeRequiredError` logs, and the current main matcher excludes that shape. I did not run a live Kiro backend in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blockers are contributor/maintainer action: narrow the recovery contract and provide real Kiro ACP behavior proof before merge.

target.title: fix(acp): recover from kiro-style SessionResumeRequiredError in persistent session resume
target.body:
## Summary

- Thread-bound ACP sessions (Kiro) get stuck after backend invalidation — messages show processing signals but never return a normal text reply
- Root cause: `isRecoverableMissingManagerPersistentSessionError` only matches `resource not found` and `no matching session`, but Kiro emits `Internal error <- SessionResumeRequiredError`
- Fix: add `internal error` to the recovery matcher regex, triggering `clearPersistedRuntimeResumeState` → `prepareFreshSession` → retry with a fresh session handle
- Fixes #87830

## Verification

- `pnpm test src/acp/control-plane/manager.turn-results.test.ts` — 16 passed (1 new regression test for Kiro `Internal error`)
- `openclaw-pr-review` — clean, no P0/P1 findings
- Autoreview: clean

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Kiro ACP persistent session threads stuck with no text reply after backend invalidation because `Internal error <- SessionResumeRequiredError` was not recognized as recoverable.

Real environment tested: Linux 4.19.112, Node 22.14, branch `fix/issue-87830-acp-session-resume-internal-error` at commit 9984a0d252, no live Kiro backend.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
pnpm test src/acp/control-plane/manager.turn-results.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix:
```console
$ node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/acp/control-plane/manager.turn-results.test.ts
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts

 RUN  v4.1.7

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  16 passed (16)
   Start at  22:22:47
   Duration  2.67s (transform 1.01s, setup 0ms, import 2.22s, tests 164ms, environment 0ms)

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 12.72s
```

Observed result after fix: All 16 tests pass, including the new test `retries once with a fresh persistent session after a kiro-style SessionResumeRequiredError with Internal error`. This test reproduces the exact Kiro error message pattern from production logs and verifies: `prepareFreshSession` is called, `ensureSession` retries without stale `resumeSessionId`, session identity updates to fresh backend session id, and state machine transitions `running -> idle` without entering `error`.

What was not tested: No live Kiro ACP backend was used; fix validated through the existing ACP mock-based test harness. The regression test uses the exact error format observed in production Kiro logs.
target.author: xialonglee
